articles.title: Grafana Confirms Breach After Hackers Claim They Stole Data
articles.summary: Grafana appears to have been targeted by Coinbase Cartel, a cybercrime group linked to ShinyHunters, Scattered Spider, and Lapsus$. The post Grafana Confirms Breach After Hackers Claim They Stole Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins
articles.summary: The flaw leads to denial-of-service on default configurations and to remote code execution if ASLR is disabled. The post Exploitation of Critical NGINX Vulnerability Begins appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: Exploit available for new DirtyDecrypt Linux root escalation flaw
articles.summary: A recently patched local privilege escalation vulnerability in the Linux kernel's rxgk module now has a proof-of-concept exploit that allows attackers to gain root access on some Linux systems. [...]

articles.title: Pre-Stuxnet Fast16 Malware Tampered with Nuclear Weapons Simulations
articles.summary: A new analysis of the Lua-based fast16 malware has confirmed that it was a cyber sabotage tool designed to tamper with nuclear weapons testing simulations. articles.title: Hackers earn $1,298,250 for 47 zero-days at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
articles.summary: The Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 hacking contest has concluded, with security researchers collecting $1,298,250 in rewards after exploiting 47 zero-day flaws. [...]

articles.title: Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
articles.summary: Participants demonstrated exploits for Windows, Linux, VMware, Nvidia, and AI products. The post Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: New Windows 'MiniPlasma' zero-day exploit gives SYSTEM access, PoC released
articles.summary: A cybersecurity researcher has released a proof-of-concept exploit for a Windows privilege escalation zero-day dubbed "MiniPlasma" that lets attackers gain SYSTEM privileges on fully patched Windows systems. [...]

articles.title: Tycoon2FA hijacks Microsoft 365 accounts via device-code phishing
articles.summary: The Tycoon2FA phishing kit now supports device-code phishing attacks and abuses Trustifi click-tracking URLs to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts. [...]

articles.summary: A newly disclosed security flaw impacting NGINX Plus and NGINX Open has come under active exploitation in the wild, days after its public disclosure, according to VulnCheck. articles.title: Grafana GitHub Token Breach Led to Codebase Download and Extortion Attempt
articles.summary: Grafana has disclosed that an "unauthorized party" obtained a token that granted them the ability to access the company's GitHub environment and download its codebase. articles.title: Microsoft rejects critical Azure vulnerability report, no CVE issued
articles.summary: A security researcher claims Microsoft quietly fixed an Azure Backup for AKS vulnerability after rejecting his report, and without issuing a CVE. Microsoft disputes the claim, telling BleepingComputer the behavior was expected and that "no product changes were made," despite the researcher documenti

articles.title: Funnel Builder Flaw Under Active Exploitation Enables WooCommerce Checkout Skimming
articles.summary: A critical security vulnerability impacting the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress has come under active exploitation in the wild to inject malicious JavaScript code into WooCommerce checkout pages with the goal of stealing payment data. articles.title: PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability
articles.summary: Introduced in 2008, the critical-severity security defect was patched this week in NGINX Plus and NGINX open source. The post PoC Code Published for Critical NGINX Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.title: More than $10 million stolen from crypto platform THORChain
articles.summary: THORChain officials said the investigation into the incident is ongoing but explained that one of their six vaults was compromised, leading to a loss of about $10.7 million.

articles.title: Funnel Builder WordPress plugin bug exploited to steal credit cards
articles.summary: A critical vulnerability in the Funnel Builder plugin for WordPress is being actively exploited to inject malicious JavaScript snippets into WooCommerce checkout pages. [...]

articles.title: Microsoft Exchange, Windows 11 hacked on second day of Pwn2Own

articles.title: Popular node-ipc npm package compromised to steal credentials
articles.summary: Hackers have injected credential-stealing malware into newly published versions of node-ipc, a popular inter-process communication package, in a new supply chain attack targeting npm. [...]

articles.summary: The Russian state-sponsored hacking group known as Turla has transformed its custom backdoor Kazuar into a modular peer-to-peer (P2P) botnet that's engineered for stealth and persistent access to compromised hosts. articles.title: CISA orders all federal agencies to patch exploited bug in Cisco SD-WAN systems by Sunday
articles.summary: Cisco released a patch for the vulnerability on Thursday, writing in an advisory that it could “allow an unauthenticated, remote attacker to bypass authentication and obtain administrative privileges on an affected system.”

articles.title: American Lending Center Data Breach Affects 123,000 Individuals
articles.summary: The non-bank lender discovered a ransomware attack nearly one year ago, but only recently completed its investigation. The post American Lending Center Data Breach Affects 123,000 Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: OpenAI has disclosed that two of its employee devices in its corporate environment were impacted via the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack on TanStack, but noted that no user data, production systems, or intellectual property were compromised or modified in an unauthorized manner. articles.title: OpenAI Hit by TanStack Supply Chain Attack
articles.summary: Two employee devices were compromised in the attack, and credential material was stolen from OpenAI code repositories. The post OpenAI Hit by TanStack Supply Chain Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: The refresh resolves critical-severity use-after-free and other types of bugs in various browser components. The post Chrome 148 Update Patches Critical Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

articles.summary: Microsoft has disclosed a new security vulnerability impacting on-premise versions of Exchange Server that it said has come under active exploitation in the wild. articles.title: TeamPCP hackers advertise Mistral AI code repos for sale
articles.summary: The TeamPCP hacker group is threatening to leak source code from the Mistral AI project unless a buyer is found for the data. [...]

articles.summary: Cisco is warning that a critical Catalyst SD-WAN Controller authentication bypass flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-20182, was actively exploited in zero-day attacks that allowed attackers to gain administrative privileges on compromised devices. [...]

articles.summary: OpenAI says two employees' devices were breached in the recent TanStack supply chain attack that impacted hundreds of npm and PyPI packages, causing the company to rotate code-signing certificates for its applications as a precaution. [...]

articles.title: ODNI taps officials to coordinate response to foreign election threats
articles.summary: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard has tapped two individuals to coordinate work across U.S. spy agencies to monitor threats to the 2026 elections, according to multiple sources familiar with the matter.

articles.summary: Cargo theft now starts with phishing emails and stolen credentials, not hijackings, to reroute and steal freight from supply chains. NMFTA outlines how cyber-enabled cargo crime is changing transportation security. [...]
